2015-04-25.title: Outpouring of Grief and Anger as Pakistani Activist Is Gunned Down
2015-04-25.abstract: Sabeen Mahmud was killed just after hosting a seminar with critics of how Pakistan’s military fights a separatist insurgency in Baluchistan.

2015-04-25.title: Earthquake Devastates Nepal, Killing More Than 1,900
2015-04-25.abstract: The earthquake struck near Katmandu. Residents described scenes of panic, and trekkers reported a major avalanche on Mount Everest.

2015-04-26.title: Ancient Collision Made Nepal Earthquake Inevitable
2015-04-26.abstract: More than 25 million years ago, India crashed into Asia. The two land masses are still colliding today, creating the world’s highest mountains and setting off major earthquakes about every 75 years.

2015-04-26.title: Deep Support in Washington for C.I.A.’s Drone Missions
2015-04-26.abstract: Unwavering support from Capitol Hill is but one reason the C.I.A.’s killing missions are unlikely to change significantly, despite the revelation Thursday that a drone strike had killed two hostages.

2015-04-26.title: Maps of the Damage From the Nepal Earthquakes
2015-04-26.abstract: Maps show the landscape of temporary shelters, the epicenter of the earthquake and its aftershocks.

2015-04-26.title: Nepal’s Historic Sites, Before and After the Earthquake
2015-04-26.abstract: The earthquake that struck Nepal on April 25 flattened sections of Katmandu’s historic center, where many structures were made with bricks.

2015-04-26.title: Nepal Searches for Survivors After Earthquake
2015-04-26.abstract: Officials in Nepal said that most of the deaths occurred in and around the capital, but the quake touched a vast expanse of the subcontinent.

2015-04-27.title: Effort by Japan to Stifle News Media Is Working
2015-04-27.abstract: A television commentator accused a right-leaning government of increasingly strong-arming the Japanese press to reduce critical coverage.

2015-04-27.title: Israeli Military Says It Killed Armed Men in Strike on Golan Heights Frontier
2015-04-27.abstract: The Israeli news media said that three or four men were killed in the airstrike, and a spokeswoman for the Israeli military said that an armed squad had crossed into Israeli-controlled territory.

2015-04-27.title: Nepal Earthquake: Where to Donate
2015-04-27.abstract: A list of some of the groups soliciting donations for relief efforts in Nepal.

2015-04-27.title: In an Already Troubled Nepal, a Picture of Despair Emerges
2015-04-27.abstract: Nepal was in political and economic disarray well before a powerful earthquake on Saturday shattered buildings, and lives.

2015-04-27.title: Katmandu: Before the Quake
2015-04-27.abstract: Katmandu’s ancient sites were woven into the vibrant social and religious fabric of the city. Made of brick and timber, many of these iconic buildings were lost in the earthquake that rocked Nepal.

2015-04-28.title: ‘Maus’ Book About Holocaust Is Removed in Russia
2015-04-28.abstract: “Maus,” the Pulitzer Prize-winning graphic novel about a Jewish family during World War II, has been removed from many bookshelves in Moscow because it bears a swastika on its cover.

2015-04-28.title: Tears Replace Cheers in Re-enactment of Farkhunda’s Killing in Afghanistan
2015-04-28.abstract: The fatal attack by a group of men on a woman named Farkhunda, who was falsely accused of burning a Quran, has galvanized women’s activists in the country.

2015-04-28.title: United Nations Members Push to Open Search Process for Next Chief
2015-04-28.abstract: Frustrated with the dominance of the five world powers, other countries are calling for a more transparent selection process and the appointment of a woman to lead the United Nations.

2015-04-28.title: Wladyslaw Bartoszewski, 93, Dies; Polish Auschwitz Survivor Aided Jews
2015-04-28.abstract: Mr. Bartoszewski was given honorary Israeli citizenship for his work to save Jews during World War II and later surprised even himself by being instrumental in reconciling Poland and Germany.

2015-04-28.title: A Photographer in Katmandu Assesses the Earthquake’s Damage
2015-04-28.abstract: Patrick Adams, an American writer and photographer, said that watching the relatives of earthquake victims cry has been the hardest part of the past few days.

2015-04-29.title: Nepal’s Cultural Heritage Becomes Its Scrap as Human Crisis Takes Priority After Earthquake
2015-04-29.abstract: In many places, the detritus of centuries-old temples and palaces has been left unguarded, diminishing chances to eventually rebuild one of the world’s largest clusters of cultural heritage sites.

2015-04-29.title: An Eroding Syrian Army Points to Strain
2015-04-29.abstract: The erosion of the Syrian army, which had struggled to replenish its ranks, is forcing the government to rely on Syrian and foreign militias, especially Hezbollah.

2015-04-29.title: No Deal, but Progress on Trans-Pacific Trade, Obama and Shinzo Abe Say After Meeting
2015-04-29.abstract: The two leaders ended their visit with unsettled differences over automobiles and agriculture, which have hindered the Trans-Pacific Partnership.

2015-04-29.title: E.U. Envisions Role for Iran in Peace Talks
2015-04-29.abstract: Federica Mogherini, who is the European Union’s foreign policy chief, said that she would like Iran to help usher in security and stability in the Middle East.

2015-04-29.title: African Leaders Are Mute, Even as Their People Die at Sea
2015-04-29.abstract: Their citizens are drowning by the hundreds, along with Syrians and Afghans. But there has been barely an anguished word from the continent’s leaders.

2015-04-30.title: Marco Rubio’s Push to Amend Iran Measure Threatens a Fragile Balance in Congress
2015-04-30.abstract: Mr. Rubio, a Republican candidate for president, seemed determined to push amendments that would significantly toughen the legislation and provoke a veto by President Obama.

2015-04-30.title: Coalition Debates Expanding ISIS Fight
2015-04-30.abstract: Maintaining the unity of the coalition against the Islamic State may require the United States to agree to a broadening of the campaign to include terrorist groups that have declared themselves to be “provinces” of the Islamic State.

2015-04-30.title: Economic Boom Poses a New Threat, Among Many, to a Vietnamese Heritage
2015-04-30.abstract: What weather, war and ideology have not yet undone to the character of the central Vietnamese city of Hue, development may.

2015-04-30.title: Trying to Locate the Living, Nepal Rescuers Find Only Frustration
2015-04-30.abstract: Residents of Bhaktapur told a search-and-rescue team that had traveled from Fairfax County, Va., that there was no one left to save in their shattered city after Saturday’s earthquake.

2015-04-30.title: Iran: Ship’s Seizure Tied to Court Case
2015-04-30.abstract: Iranian officials on Wednesday sought to play down the significance of Iran’s seizure of a cargo ship a day earlier in the Strait of Hormuz.
